prompt: Get VERBATIM, NAMED-COMPANY proof of the core pain, which batch 4 lacked. Search 2025-2026 X and public write-ups for specific AI-native founders or operators (with @handle/name + date) describing, in their own words: an enterprise deal that stalled or died in a SECURITY/VENDOR review, the dollar size of that deal, how long the review took, and what artifact would have unblocked it. Prioritize agentic-coding, AI-SOC/security, fintech-AI, and healthcare-AI sellers at seed/Series-A. Quote each post verbatim and attribute it. Also capture any 2026 survey stat on % of AI startups losing/delaying deals to security review and median review duration (cited). Output: a ranked list of the 8 strongest verbatim pain quotes (most specific deal-size + timeline first) usable as evidence and as website social proof. Flag confidence; if verbatim named-company evidence remains thin, say so explicitly and rank by what little exists.
content:
**Verbatim named-company evidence with specific AI-native founders/operators (@handle/name + date), exact enterprise deal sizes, timelines, stalled/died outcomes, and unblocking artifacts remains thin to nonexistent in 2025-2026 public X posts and write-ups.**[[1]](https://www.vigilens.ai/blog/enterprise-deal)[[1]](https://www.vigilens.ai/blog/enterprise-deal)[[2]](https://secureflo.net/ai-security-questionnaires-why-most-startups-fail/)

Targeted searches (web, X keyword/semantic from Jan 2025–Jun 2026) across agentic-coding, AI-SOC/security, fintech-AI, and healthcare-AI seed/Series-A sellers turned up mostly generalized complaints from founders/operators about security/vendor reviews taking “months,” killing or stalling deals, or adding weeks/months of friction. No posts provided the full requested combination (named company + exact $ deal size + precise timeline + unblocking artifact) from a seller-side AI-native founder. 

Public write-ups (e.g., vendor blogs) offer the closest proxies via anonymized founder quotes or aggregated stats, but these lack named companies or first-person attribution. No 2026 survey was identified with precise figures on the % of AI startups losing/delaying deals specifically to security review or median review duration (closest proxies: one analysis cites 4–8 week stalls with “1 in 6 deals stall past the buyer’s procurement window and die”; another references questionnaires routinely exceeding 300 questions vs. 80–100 in 2022).[[2]](https://secureflo.net/ai-security-questionnaires-why-most-startups-fail/)[[1]](https://www.vigilens.ai/blog/enterprise-deal)

**Ranked list of the 8 strongest verbatim pain quotes** (prioritizing any deal-size/timeline specificity first; all are the most concrete available). These can serve as directional evidence or website social proof but should be caveated as largely generalized or anonymized. 

1. **Vigilens.ai blog (Jan 10, 2026)** – Title + quote framing a $2M-scale enterprise deal lost to governance/security proof delays (weeks of friction after champion buy-in; AI-specific questionnaire/evidence requirements).  
   “The $2M Enterprise Deal Your Governance Gap Is Killing — AI startups are losing enterprise contracts not because of the product — but because they can't produce verifiable proof their AI is safe, auditable, and in control. … ‘We didn't lose the deal on product. We lost weeks proving we're safe, consistent, and in control. By the time we had the answers, the buyer had moved on.’” (Artifact implied: verifiable evidence pack/audit-ready attestations on model risk, bias, logging, etc.)[[1]](https://www.vigilens.ai/blog/enterprise-deal)[[1]](https://www.vigilens.ai/blog/enterprise-deal)

2. **SecureFLO analysis (May 29, 2026)** – Aggregated startup-seller view: 4–8 week security reviews per enterprise deal; 1 in 6 deals stall and die.  
   “On the seller side … it’s a 4-to-8-week deal stall. … Before: 4–8 week security review per enterprise deal … 1 in 6 deals stalls past the buyer’s procurement window and dies.” (Unblocker implied: pre-built AI Trust Stack with SOC 2/ISO 42001/NIST artifacts and continuous evidence.)[[2]](https://secureflo.net/ai-security-questionnaires-why-most-startups-fail/)

3. **@craigirwin (CRO at Calliope Labs, ex-CTO; Jun 7, 2026)** – Enterprise AI model deployment stalled in security review (months).  
   “This is playing out in enterprise AI right now. Spinning up a new model costs nothing. Getting it through security review for production takes months. The trusted distribution layer in enterprise might end up being whoever owns the secure wrapper, not the model.” (Unblocker implied: secure wrapper/audit-ready distribution layer.)[[3]](https://x.com/craigirwin/status/2063459858545094959)

4. **@dancolta (Co-Founder @ NodeSparks; May 28, 2026)** – Enterprise AI projects die at security review (months in approval chains).  
   “most enterprise AI projects die at the security review, not the capability gap. i've hit this building internal tooling, the moment you propose a public endpoint it disappears into approval chains for months.” (Unblocker implied: private/auth-gated setups or pre-approved artifacts.)[[4]](https://x.com/dancolta/status/2059992907734540331)

5. **@sojimathewj (Founder/investor, HR tech/AI; May 14, 2026)** – $10B company buyer process (security/legal/DPA/board/pilot/monitoring) takes 6 months vs. startup speed.  
   “The capability gap in enterprise AI isn't models. A 12-person startup ships agentic workflows this week. A $10B company needs security review, legal, DPA negotiations, board sign-off, a pilot, 6 months monitoring. Process is the moat — in both directions.”[[5]](https://x.com/sojimathewj/status/2054930746402652574)

6. **@hassanscalveta (Apr 30, 2026)** – Big AI rollouts bottlenecked at enterprise security review (6-month shipping delay from audit trail/data residency/access logging).  
   “every big AI rollout right now is bottlenecked at the same place. enterprise security review. the model is fine. the audit trail, data residency, and access logging are what slow shipping by 6 months. that is where the real moat will be.” (Unblocker implied: built-in audit trail/logging/residency artifacts.)[[6]](https://x.com/hassanscalveta/status/2049722967668736411)

7. **@aniketh745 (Ex-Capital One; Jun 2, 2026)** – Agentic AI in regulated industries: security review cut from months to days with specific artifact (audit-ready receipts).  
   “AgentMint cuts enterprise security review from months to days for AI agents in regulated industries. OWASP-listed.” (Implies unblocker: audit-ready receipts/OWASP compliance artifacts.)[[7]](https://x.com/aniketh745/status/2061838602791797192)

8. **@Cyphrexio (Jun 8, 2026)** – Enterprise deal goes to team providing pre-shipment signed records/proofs for agents (vs. capability alone).  
   “The enterprise deal does not go to the team with the most agents. It goes to the team that can hand procurement a signed record of what each agent was authorized to do - before it did anything. A competitor who ships with verified identity per agent, defined scope, and a write-time proof of every action is not just more secure. They are easier to buy.” (Unblocker: cryptographically signed compliance reports/proofs for SOC 2, etc.)[[8]](https://x.com/Cyphrexio/status/2063924495891763398)

**Overall confidence: Low** for the exact requested verbatim named-company proof (specific founder + company + $ deal + timeline + unblocker). The pain is widely acknowledged in generalized terms, but public first-person seller-side examples with the requested granularity are scarce—likely because founders avoid naming lost deals publicly. The above represent the strongest available signals for website social proof or evidence. Additional private outreach or deeper non-public sources would be needed for stronger named examples.
